channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump orders US military to shoot Iranian boats laying mines in Hormuz Strait
item.description: President Trump has ordered the US Navy to shoot and kill any Iranian boats deploying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating the standoff as Iran continues to disrupt traffic through the critical waterway. The order came after Iran laid mines for a second time this week.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan blames US blockade, not Iranian divisions, for stalled ceasefire talks
item.description: Pakistani officials say the US naval blockade of Iranian ports is preventing Tehran from attending talks in Islamabad, contradicting Trump who cited Irans "fractured" government. The diplomatic stalemate continues as both sides set preconditions for negotiations.

item.title: 🏗️ Trump administration pitches US companies for Gulf reconstruction contracts
item.description: The US is lobbying Gulf states to use American firms to rebuild infrastructure damaged by Iranian strikes, pressing Kuwait, Bahrain and the UAE. An Arab official called the push "tone-deaf" given the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Indias Reliance Industries profits hit by Iran war supply shock
item.description: Billionaire Mukesh Ambanis conglomerate reported a year-on-year profit decline for the final quarter, as supply disruptions from the US-Iran war impacted oil and gas operations at Indias largest company.

item.title: ⛪ US lawmakers introduce bill to counter Russias religious repression in Ukraine
item.description: A bipartisan group in Congress introduced the Countering Russias War on Faith Act, requiring State and Defense departments to report on Moscows persecution of religious groups in Ukraine and authorizing sanctions against those responsible.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Soldier Charged for Using Classified Intel to Bet on Maduro Raid
item.description: A US soldier was charged with using classified intelligence to place a $400,000 Polymarket bet on the Venezuela raid, raising serious security concerns about insider trading on military operations.

item.title: 🛢️ Russian Oil Exports Slump as Ukraine Hammers Ports and Refineries
item.description: Russian oil exports are projected to fall to their lowest levels since 2023 as Ukrainian drone strikes continue targeting ports and refineries, despite a US sanctions waiver.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Leaders Eye Mutual Defense Pact Amid Trump NATO Uncertainty
item.description: European Union nations are examining a little-known mutual assistance obligation as an alternative to NATO, as Trump continues to criticize the alliance and European defense commitments.

item.title: 🇽🇰 Kosovo Court Jails Three Serb Separatists Over 2023 Banjska Attack
item.description: A Kosovo court sentenced three Serb separatists for the 2023 Banjska attack that left one police officer and three gunmen dead, a key verdict in the volatile Kosovo-Serbia conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Soldiers Left Emaciated on Frontline From Lack of Food and Water
item.description: Ukrainian soldiers on the frontline are suffering from severe malnutrition due to critical supply shortages, highlighting the dire logistical challenges facing Kyiv's forces.

item.title: 🇪🇺 NATO says no mechanism to expel members after US reportedly considers suspending Spain
item.description: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over perceived lack of support for the Iran war. NATO clarified there is no provision for expelling members from the alliance.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK reaffirms Falklands sovereignty after report of US policy review
item.description: Downing Street stated Falklands sovereignty rests with the UK after an internal Pentagon document reportedly raised the prospect of a position change in retaliation for Britain not joining the Iran war.

item.title: 🏛️ US Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed Chair Jerome Powell
item.description: The DOJ ended its investigation into the Federal Reserve chairman after President Trump accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed building. The move clears the path for Powell successor Kevin Warsh confirmation.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Mandelson under formal investigation by EU anti-fraud office
item.description: The European Anti-Fraud Office confirmed it has sufficient information to launch an investigation into the UK trade minister, though it has not specified whether the probe involves fraud allegations.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump to send Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for Iran talks
item.description: US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will fly to Pakistan for discussions on Iran, while Irans foreign minister is in Islamabad. Iranian state media reports direct US talks are not on the agenda.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇸🇦 Zelensky makes second Saudi Arabia visit in less than a month for security-energy talks
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is advancing agreements with Saudi Arabia in security, energy, and infrastructure during his second visit to the kingdom in under a month.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine brings home 193 POWs from Russian captivity
item.description: The prisoners returned include individuals against whom Russian authorities had opened criminal cases, as well as wounded soldiers. Russia also received 193 POWs in exchange.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 Trump cannot expel NATO members over Iran stance, alliance official says
item.description: A NATO official clarified that the US president lacks authority to unilaterally suspend alliance members, amid reports that the Pentagon considered punishing Spain and others for not backing the Iran war.

item.title: 🇨🇳🤖 Iran war shows strategic value of AI for Chinese military, PLA Daily says
item.description: Chinese state media highlighted the Iran conflict as demonstrating artificial intelligence's growing importance in modern warfare, emphasizing lessons for the People's Liberation Army.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu diagnosed with prostate cancer without informing Israeli public
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was diagnosed with prostate cancer but did not disclose the information to the public, Haaretz reports.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Justice Department drops probe into Federal Reserve Chair Powell
item.description: The DOJ dropped its investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell after Trump accused him of cost overruns in renovating the Fed building. The move clears the path for confirmation of his successor Kevin Warsh.

item.title: 🏴‍☠️ Pirates hijack oil tanker off coast of Somalia
item.description: A commercial oil tanker has been hijacked by pirates off the Somali coast, marking a resurgence of piracy in the Indian Ocean. The incident comes amid heightened global shipping concerns due to the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🐺 18 wolves found dead from suspected poisoning in Italian national park
item.description: Italian authorities are investigating the deaths of 18 wolves in a national park, with poisoning suspected. Ten carcasses were found last week and another eight in recent days.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Three US aircraft carriers operate in Middle East for first time since 2003
item.description: The US military has three aircraft carrier strike groups in the Middle East simultaneously for the first time since 2003, amid fragile truce negotiations with Iran. The USS George H.W. Bush joins existing carriers as tensions remain high.

item.title: 🇵🇸 Gaza to hold first local election in two decades
item.description: Residents of Deir al-Balah are set to vote in municipal elections, the first in Gaza in 20 years. Hamas is not participating in the elections scheduled for this weekend.
